reviews.review_msg: I found Jade's class to be interesting and engaging. It was my favourite course for Spring 2016. I'm dissapointed that SFU did not hire her. I would love to take more courses with her. She is passionate about what she teaches and has lots of good real life advice about Qualitative research.
Great and helpful professor! It can be a easy course if take with her. Enjoy the research term paper - can choose your own research topic
Great and helpful professor! It can be a easy course if take with her. Enjoy the research term paper - can choose your own research topic
Great and helpful professor! It can be a easy course if take with her. Enjoy the research term paper - can choose your own research topic
This prof is very funny and super cute too. Great class.
This prof is very funny and super cute too. Great class.
This prof is very funny and super cute too. Great class.

Dr. Shaw is the BEST instructor in the SA department. She is very engaged and organized during her lectures. I found her class to be very relevant to current issues. Each class is exciting as she makes learning fun! Her assignments are graded fairly with clear rubrics to follow. Her use of Canvas is well organized and she posts ALL lectures.

I THINK IT IS TIME TO RETIRE

Great professor! The class was actually SA443 - very discussion-based class, really interesting content and she taught it well. Completely open to student feedback and is there to help when needed. Would definitely take again!

AVOID AT ALL COSTS!!! NO KIDDING!! IF YOU CARE FOR YOUR SANITY AND PEACE, PLEASE AVOID HER! She does NOT teach!  Students do their own learning and when she "teaches" her explanations are terrible and incomprehensible! Her last name even says it ALL! Please please please avoid this lecturer and if unfortunately, you can't, well sorry and GOODLUCK!
She requires a lot of work with no help or clear direction Multiple assignments due a week with lots of readings.
Avoid at all costs

Guidelines for assignments are vague.  Encourages discussion, but lectures can be dull nonetheless.  Avoid her if you don't need SA356.  Marking scheme for the course includes 4 fieldnote journals, 4 assignments based on those fieldnotes, attendance/participation, 3 methods summaries for the readings, and a Pechakucha presentation.
She is a horrible professor who is incredible ignorant. She ignores valid opinions made by her students and attempts to belittle them whenever possible. I strongly recommend avoiding her.

Pam is AMAZING! Super engaging and funny. Heavy course load (we're working on a collaborative research project as a class) but all of the assignments have been very useful and practical. Pam's so helpful and provides incredible feedback on all assignments. Favourite prof at SFU thus far. I highly recommend taking a class with Pam.
I THINK IT IS TIME TO RETIRE

My experience with Kathleen was incredible! She is incredibly supportive of all of her students, and engages you with class content. One of my favourite professors in the sociology department by far.

for God's sake woman let us use laptops in class. It's an incredible resource for students to look up terminology and theories that you're mentioning AND FAILING to explain comprehensively. Take that Gen X stick out and welcome the new age of learning. Four hours of your unorganized rambling isn't educational.

Lots of group work that seemed aimless, with very little actual instruction on the course topics. Criteria varied considerably between the prof and the TM, which made constructive feedback hard to find.

As others have noted, she does seem to have good intentions and is excited about teaching, but sadly, she is very very bad at it. Lots of useless group work. Lots of small assignments and "field projects" that require work and dont really have a point. Large assignment ok but lectures are painful!!!
Good professor!  Cares about what she teaches.  She encourages participation and teaches critical thinking.
